# server/standard-schema-bridge

> **Server Factory Layer**

Bridge adapting Effect [Schema](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) instances into the
`StandardSchemaWithJSON` interface consumed by the MCP SDK.

## Remarks

The MCP SDK's `registerTool()` expects tool input schemas to satisfy the
`StandardSchemaWithJSON` contract — an object whose `~standard` property
exposes both a `validate` function and a `jsonSchema` accessor.  Effect
Schema does not implement this interface natively, so this module
provides a thin adapter ([toStandardSchema](functions/toStandardSchema.md)) that bridges the two
worlds.

The adapter delegates to:

- [Schema.decodeUnknownEither](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) — for runtime validation, producing
  either a decoded value or structured validation issues.
- [JSONSchema.make](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) — for the JSON Schema representation that the
  SDK serializes into the tool's metadata.

## See

[Standard Schema specification](https://github.com/standard-schema/standard-schema)

## Functions

| Function                                          | Description                                                                                                                                                   |
| ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [toStandardSchema](functions/toStandardSchema.md) | Adapts an Effect [Schema.Schema](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) into the `StandardSchemaWithJSON` interface required by the MCP SDK's `registerTool()`. |
